Okay nvm, lets just make a new one - heres the problem. upon you running gateway restart - there is a message thats sent when it restarts that acknowledges the restart "hot restart" is what the system calls it - but the problem is, we never actually get the message. theres 2 things about this.

1) if im on the web ui and call for a gateway restart within a chat session, when it comes back online - that message should appear in the same chat session! 

2) if im on telegram calling for a gateway restart, same thing! i should get the restart message on telegram

I used that and created the proposal concept accordingly:

### Proposal Title
**Guarantee hot-restart acknowledgment delivery to originating web session or Telegram chat**

### Summary
Ensure the post-`gateway_restart` “hot restart” acknowledgment is always delivered back to the exact origin channel/context:  
1) same **web UI chat session** if restart was triggered there,  
2) same **Telegram chat** if restart was triggered there.

### Details (implementation intent)
- Persist restart origin metadata before shutdown (channel + session/chat routing identifiers + correlation id).
- On boot, consume pending hot-restart context and emit exactly one acknowledgment to the correct destination:
  - web: append system message into the same chat session thread
  - telegram: send message to the initiating telegram conversation
- Add retry/fallback handling so acknowledgments are not silently lost during early boot race conditions.
- Mark pending restart notice as delivered (idempotent guard) to prevent duplicates on subsequent restarts.
- Include structured logs for traceability (`restart_id`, origin, delivery result, latency).

If you want, I’ll now submit this as a formal proposal immediately.
